name: Feedback Intent
summary: Protocol defining transient, non-blocking messaging (Toasts/Snackbars) and their queuing logic. Tone dimension participates in the unified tone vocabulary shared with Message and Validation intents (#1427 canonical names).

tone.description: Semantic weight of the message — the canonical `tone` vocabulary (#1427, renamed from `severity`). Synonyms normalize to one token: info (informational), success (confirmation; ≡ positive), warning (advisory; ≡ caution), danger (failure; ≡ error ≡ negative). neutral and info stay distinct.

description:
Placement Logic

  • top-end: Standard for Desktop notifications (Toasts).
  • bottom-start: Standard for Material Design (Snackbars). Avoids FAB interaction.
  • top-center: Mobile alerts.

Tone Semantics

tone is part of a unified tone vocabulary (#1427 canonical names, renamed from severity) shared across Feedback (transient), Message (structural), and Validation (input states). All three use the same semantic set:

  • info: Informational. Neutral tone. No action required.
  • success: Confirmation. Positive outcome achieved.
  • warning: Advisory. Issue detected but not blocking. User may act.
  • danger: Failure. Problem occurred. User action required (≡ error ≡ negative).

Timing & Duration

  • short: ~4000ms. For simple confirmations ("Saved").
  • long: ~10000ms. If text > 60 chars.
  • indefinite: Remains until dismissed. Required if an Action is present (Accessibility).

Interface Protocol

type FeedbackPlacement = 'top-start' | 'top-end' | 'bottom-start' | 'bottom-end' | 'top-center' | 'bottom-center';
type FeedbackTone = 'info' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'danger';

interface FeedbackAction {
    label: string;
    intent: string;
}

interface FeedbackIntent {
    // Positioning strategy
    placement: FeedbackPlacement;
    // Semantic weight (shared tone vocabulary, #1427)
    tone: FeedbackTone;
    // Persistence
    duration: 'short' | 'long' | 'indefinite';
    // Queue Management (1 = Single/Replace, >1 = Stack)
    stackLimit: number;
    // Optional interactive element (e.g. "Undo")
    action?: FeedbackAction;
}
